stories.headline: [AP News] Australian families leave Syria camp linked to Islamic State militants
stories.summary: A group of Australian families with ties to former Islamic State fighters have left a Syrian refugee camp, attempting to return to Australia. Eleven families, comprising approximately 34 women and children, departed the Al Roj camp near Damascus, planning to travel to Beirut before heading to Australia. However, a previous attempt by 34 Australians, including 11 women and 23 children, was reportedly forced to head back. Currently, seven Australian women and 14 children remain in the al-Roj camp. The wives, widows, and children of jailed or dead Islamic State fighters have been held in the camp for years. Kurdish-led SDF forces escorted the group towards Damascus, but their journey to Australia is ongoing, with some having already left and others still awaiting departure.

stories.headline: [The Straits Times] Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
stories.summary: Malaysia has appointed a former high court judge as its new anti-graft chief, replacing Azam Baki who faced allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding. The appointment comes after a probe into Azam's shareholdings, which had sparked renewed scrutiny and protests. Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim had been poised to drop Azam, according to people familiar with the matter. The outgoing chief had been dogged by allegations, including claims of MACC infiltration, and the King had warned against politicising the issue. The new appointment aims to address the concerns and restore public trust in the anti-graft agency. The change in leadership is seen as a significant development in Malaysia's efforts to tackle corruption and ensure accountability. The former high court judge is expected to lead the agency in a new direction.

stories.headline: [CNA] Iran's foreign minister arrives in Pakistan, Trump expects offer satisfying US demands
stories.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi arrived in Islamabad on Friday to discuss proposals for restarting peace talks with the US. The visit offers hope for an end to the eight-week conflict that has resulted in significant loss of life. Araqchi's trip is aimed at resolving US demands, with US President Trump expecting an offer that satisfies American requirements. According to Pakistan government sources, Iran is willing to make a deal and is planning to present a proposal. The US and Iran have been at odds for eight weeks, and a potential breakthrough in talks could lead to a peaceful resolution. The meeting in Islamabad is seen as a crucial step in restarting negotiations between the two nations. Trump has indicated that Iran wants to talk and is open to making a deal.

stories.headline: [Moneycontrol.com] US Navy destroyer enforces Iran port blockade, halts Iranian-flagged ship
stories.summary: A US Navy destroyer enforced a blockade of Iranian ports, stopping an Iranian-flagged ship named TOUSKA. The ship, nearly 900 feet long, attempted to breach the blockade but was halted. The US naval blockade, in place since earlier in April, restricts shipping to and from Iranian ports. The blockade is being enforced against ships of any nationality and is tracking vessels heading to or from Iran. The move has disrupted global oil and LNG supplies. Iranian officials have warned that the crucial shipping lane will remain shut until the US lifts the blockade. The blockade is part of stalled US-Iran negotiations, with the US stating it will remain in place until a deal is reached. The situation has raised concerns about the risks of the blockade and its impact on global trade.

stories.headline: Your Windows Restart button is getting an update - now it does what you tell it to
stories.summary: Microsoft is updating the Windows Restart button to give users more control over when their device restarts. The update aims to reduce forced restarts, allowing users to choose when to apply updates. Previously, Windows would often restart without warning, potentially causing inconvenience. With the new update, users can schedule a restart time that suits them, providing more flexibility. This change is part of the Windows Update controls, which also include the ability to download and install updates at a later time. The update is intended to address issues such as the "blue screen of death" and endless reboot loops, which can occur when a device is stuck in a restart cycle. Users can access the new controls by going to Settings > Windows Update > Check for updates.

stories.headline: Apple's 20th anniversary iPhone to use quad-curved display, new rumor claims
stories.summary: Apple's 20th-anniversary iPhone is expected to feature a quad-curved display, according to recent rumors. The company is reportedly working with Samsung to develop the screen, which will have a customized curvature radius and no polarizer. The quad-curved design will bend the display around all four sides of the device, creating a bezel-less look. This design is a significant departure from previous iPhones and is expected to be a key feature of the 2027 release. Prototype designs have already been created, showcasing a seamless and borderless look. The use of a quad-curved panel is not new in the industry, but it would be a first for Apple's iPhone line. The collaboration with Samsung on the display technology is also notable, as the two companies have worked together on previous projects. The new design is expected to be a major update for the iPhone.

stories.headline: vivo unveils Y6 5G featuring 7,200 mAh battery and Snapdragon 4 Gen 2 SoC
stories.summary: Vivo has launched the Y6 5G smartphone in China, featuring a large 7,200mAh battery with 44W wired charging. The device is powered by the Snapdragon 4 Gen 2 SoC and comes with a 120Hz LCD screen and a 50MP rear camera. The Y6 5G offers up to 12GB of RAM and has IP68/IP69 durability ratings. The phone is available in silver and other colors, with pricing starting from CNY 1,799, approximately ₹24,000. The Y6 5G is the latest addition to Vivo's budget-friendly Y-series lineup, offering a significant battery capacity and fast charging capabilities. The device's specs and features position it as a competitive option in the budget smartphone market.

stories.headline: Nintendo's first authorized store in PH to open in Makati this May
stories.summary: Nintendo is opening its first authorized store in the Philippines at SM Makati in May 2026. The store will be a significant development for local gamers, who have previously relied on third-party and online options. This will be the company's first authorized retail store in the country, providing a dedicated space for Nintendo products and services. The announcement was made by SM Makati on Facebook, featuring a photo of a sign with Mario's face and the words "coming soon." The store's opening is expected to bring a more official and direct shopping experience for Nintendo Switch users and fans in the Philippines, offering a range of Nintendo products and potentially other services. The exact opening date in May has not been specified.

stories.headline: Nathan Lukes exits with injury TORONTO -- Another day, another injury for the Blue Jays. This time, it was Nathan Lukes leaving the game early with left hamstring discomfort after running out
stories.summary: Toronto Blue Jays outfielder Nathan Lukes exited a game against the Cleveland Guardians with left hamstring discomfort. The injury occurred after Lukes hit a double in the first inning of Friday's 8-6 loss. He was forced to leave the game early due to the discomfort. This is the latest in a series of injuries for the Blue Jays. The team announced that Lukes' early exit was caused by left hamstring discomfort, but did not provide further details on the severity of the injury or a potential timeline for his return. The Blue Jays are dealing with multiple injuries early in the season, and Lukes' status will be monitored moving forward. The team will likely provide updates on his condition as more information becomes available.

stories.headline: Skip Bayless, NBA world react as LeBron James, Lakers near Rockets sweep with Game 3 win In a nerve-racking overtime crunch time in Houston, the Los Angeles Lakers dismantled the Rockets with a 112-108 Game 3 win of their best-of-seven first-round
stories.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers took a 3-0 series lead against the Houston Rockets with a 112-108 Game 3 win in overtime. LeBron James scored 29 points, including a tying three-pointer with 13 seconds left in regulation. The Lakers opened overtime with a 6-2 run, led by a three-pointer from Smart, to take a 107-103 lead. Smart later made two free throws to push the lead to 109-105 with 52 seconds left. The Lakers made their first seven shots and were 11-for-16 from the field and 6-for-8 on 3s at one point. The win puts the Lakers on the verge of a sweep in the best-of-seven first-round series. The Rockets' loss sparked an angry reaction from Tari Eason's mother, while the NBA world, including Skip Bayless, reacted to the Lakers' dominant performance.

stories.headline: Jaden McDaniels is simply motivated by seeing Denver on the court. Minnesota is reaping the benefits Jaden McDaniels pushed himself into the spotlight in the first round of the NBA playoffs with his declaration following Minnesota’s comeback victory in Game 2
stories.summary: Jaden McDaniels has been motivated by facing the Denver Nuggets in the NBA playoffs, which has benefited the Minnesota Timberwolves. Following Minnesota's comeback victory in Game 2, McDaniels stated that the key to offensive success in the series is driving to the basket and directly attacking Denver. McDaniels, a forward for the Timberwolves, played a significant role in the team's performance. The series is part of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs, with Game 2 taking place at Ball Arena in Denver, Colorado. McDaniels' declaration and performance have pushed him into the spotlight, highlighting his importance to the Timberwolves' success in the playoffs. The team is reaping the benefits of his motivation, with McDaniels' efforts contributing to their strong showing against the Nuggets.

stories.headline: Bills select CB Davison Igbinosun Bills select CB Davison Igbinosun | WGR Sports Radio Buffalo
stories.summary: The Buffalo Bills selected cornerback Davison Igbinosun from Ohio State with the 62nd pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. The team traded up four spots, from pick 66 to pick 62, to secure Igbinosun. This move indicates the Bills' emphasis on strengthening their secondary, particularly given the frequency of injuries to their returning starters. Igbinosun is described as a big press-man cornerback, suggesting he will play a key role in the team's defensive strategy. The selection was made in the second round of the draft, highlighting the Bills' priority on bolstering their defense with talented young players. The trade and selection demonstrate the team's proactive approach to addressing potential vulnerabilities and building a competitive roster.

stories.headline: UBSE result 2026 out LIVE: Uttarakhand class 10 and 12 results out on ubse.uk.gov.in; two girls share top rank in 12th
stories.summary: The Uttarakhand Board of School Education (UBSE) has declared the results for class 10 and 12 exams. The overall pass percentage for class 12 is 85.11%, with girls performing better at 88.09% and boys at 81.93%. For class 10, the pass percentage is 92.10%, with girls at 96.07% and boys at 88.03%. Geetika Pant from Bageshwar and Sushila Mendiratta from Udham Singh Nagar have shared the top rank in the class 12 exams. Students from hilly regions outperformed their urban counterparts. The results are available on the UBSE website, DigiLocker, and school-level result portals. The state education minister, Dhan Singh Rawat, announced the results in a press conference on April 25 at 10 AM. Candidates can now access their scores through these multiple channels.

stories.headline: ‘He seemed calm, sweet, apologetic’: Hotel manager on Kailash Hills rape-murder accused Rahul Meena
stories.summary: Rahul Meena, the 23-year-old accused in the Kailash Hills rape-murder case, appeared calm and composed during interactions with a hotel manager and police. The manager reported that Meena seemed sweet and apologetic when checking into a hotel, stating he was from Alwar and in Delhi for work. He provided his Aadhaar ID number when asked for identification. Meena's demeanor was described as normal and polite. During police questioning, he maintained a calm and composed demeanor, claiming he had only gone to the victim's home to get money. Police traced Meena through CCTV footage, leading to his arrest. The accused's calm behavior has been consistently noted by those who interacted with him, despite the severity of the crime he is accused of committing. Meena's actions and statements are being investigated further by the authorities.

stories.headline: EC suspends five Diamond Harbour cops for alleged misconduct during Bengal assembly elections
stories.summary: The Election Commission suspended five police officers in Diamond Harbour, West Bengal, for alleged misconduct during the assembly elections. The action was taken due to "serious misconduct" and failure to maintain neutrality. The suspended officers are from the Diamond Harbour Police District. Additionally, the Commission issued a warning to Diamond Harbour SP Ishani Pal for failing to ensure discipline and fairness among subordinate officers. The West Bengal government was directed to initiate disciplinary action against the suspended officials. The suspension comes after Phase 1 of the Bengal elections, which saw a record 92.6% turnout. The Election Commission's decision aims to ensure fairness and neutrality in the electoral process. The suspended officers will face disciplinary action, and the Commission will continue to monitor the situation to prevent similar incidents in future phases of the election.

stories.headline: Delhi's Kailash Hills horror: Police to question family of accused in rape-murder case
stories.summary: Delhi Police are investigating the rape and murder of a 22-year-old woman in Kailash Hills, with a focus on questioning the family of the accused, 23-year-old Rahul Meena. Meena was arrested on Wednesday and preliminary interrogation suggests the motive may have been "revenge or a grudge" against the victim's family. Crime and forensic teams recreated the crime scene and are widening the probe to include Meena's family members, including his father in Alwar and uncle in Gurugram. The police have confirmed that Meena was seen outside the house just days before the incident, which occurred around 6:39 am when he entered the house and murdered the victim, the daughter of a civil servant and a dentist. The police are working to ascertain the motive behind the crime.

stories.headline: US and China Engage in High-Stakes Diplomacy Amid Rising Tensions
stories.summary: The US and China are engaging in high-stakes diplomacy amid rising tensions. Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are set to meet in South Korea, with trade negotiations being a key focus. The US has threatened a 100% tariff on Chinese goods, while China has been showcasing its military capabilities, including the deployment of its amphibious warship, the Sichuan, in waters where the US, Japan, and the Philippines are conducting joint exercises. The meeting between Trump and Xi aims to ease tensions and make progress on trade talks. The two countries have been seeking an upper hand in negotiations, with the US pushing for better trade terms and China looking to protect its interests. The outcome of the meeting is crucial, given the significant economic and geopolitical implications of the US-China relationship. Recent developments suggest a potential breakthrough in the talks.

stories.headline: NATO Leaders Gather for Emergency Meeting to Discuss Russia's Aggression
stories.summary: NATO leaders are gathering for an emergency meeting to discuss Russia's aggression, particularly its recent actions in relation to Iran. Russia has delivered the S-500 air defense system to Iran, prompting the extraordinary meeting of the North Atlantic Council at the level of Ministers of Foreign Affairs in Brussels. The meeting comes amid heightened tensions between Russia and NATO, which have been strained since Russia's annexation of Crimea and invasion of eastern Ukraine in 2014. The alliance is also set to debate the ongoing war in Ukraine and revise plans for dealing with Russian aggression. The US has been pushing for increased defense spending from NATO member states, with a goal of 5% of GDP, while also seeking a swift end to the conflict in Ukraine.

stories.headline: Global Economic Forum Warns of Looming Trade War Between Major World Powers
stories.summary: The World Economic Forum (WEF) has warned of a looming trade war between major world powers, posing the biggest threat to global stability in 2026. According to the WEF's Global Risks Report, escalating economic confrontation between major powers is now seen as the greatest risk facing the world over the next two years. The think tank's report draws on expert opinions, highlighting trade wars and economic disputes as key concerns. The warning comes after a year of heightened uncertainty and trade tensions, with the conflict between Iran and Israel, and the involvement of the United States, temporarily shifting focus away from trade and tariffs. The WEF is urging policymakers and business leaders to respond with greater coordination, strategic agility, and investment in transformative technologies like artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Separation between Economics and Strategy Has Collapsed in the 21st Century
stories.summary: Pradeep Mehta stated that the separation between economics and strategy has collapsed in the 21st century. This assertion was made during a webinar where four papers presented strategic thinking from various regions and economies. Mehta emphasized the importance of getting geoeconomics right, citing examples such as Taiwan's plans to mass-produce 2-nanometre chips by late 2025. The convergence of economics and strategy is driven by technological advancements and global shifts, including the energy transition to renewables and electrification. As the world undergoes significant changes, the distinction between economic and strategic decision-making has become increasingly blurred, requiring a more integrated approach to navigate the complexities of the 21st century. This shift is expected to define the era, much like coal and steam power defined the 19th century and oil and mass production defined the 20th century.

stories.headline: D-Street bears brunt of elusive US-Iran truce Indian stock markets closed the week with losses
stories.summary: Indian stock markets closed the week with losses due to fading optimism for a US-Iran truce, impacting investor sentiment. The absence of a truce led to a significant spike in crude oil prices. Additionally, IT companies reported lower-than-expected earnings, contributing to the market decline. Earlier, Indian stock markets had surged following a ceasefire announcement, with Sensex rising 2,625 points to 77,300 and Nifty gaining 773 points to 23,914. However, the markets erased these gains, resulting in a 2% weekly fall. The Nifty and Sensex indices were affected by the IT sector's weakness and the escalation of geopolitical tensions, ultimately leading to a bearish trend in the market. The weekly losses reflect the market's sensitivity to global events and earnings reports.

stories.headline: Optimism for a US-Iran truce faded, impacting investor sentiment
stories.summary: Optimism for a US-Iran truce has faded, impacting investor sentiment and causing crude oil prices to spike. The US had signaled cautious optimism for renewed peace talks in Pakistan, despite high tensions and economic stakes. However, an official announced that the US military has paused strikes on Iran. Iran's foreign minister stated that the country's military will coordinate the passage of vessels through the critical Strait of Hormuz during the ceasefire. The situation remains volatile, with the US and Iran maintaining a cautious stance. The economic impact is being felt, with IT companies reporting lower-than-expected earnings. The situation is being closely monitored, with news outlets providing real-time updates and expert analysis on the developing story. The US and Iran continue to navigate a fragile ceasefire, with the international community watching for any signs of escalation or resolution.

stories.headline: Crude oil prices saw a significant spike
stories.summary: Crude oil prices have experienced a significant spike. The current market is awaiting the outcome of ongoing talks, which may impact the price trajectory. Brent Crude and WTI oil futures are being closely monitored, with price charts and real-time quotes available. The limited supply of crude oil, combined with growing demand, has contributed to the continuous rise in oil prices. Crude oil is not only used as a fuel source but also in the production of cosmetics and medicines. As of April 2026, the price of Brent Oil Futures for June 2026 is being tracked, with technical analysis, news, and forecasts providing insight into potential future price movements. The crude oil market remains volatile, with downside risk and potential for counter-trend bounces, making it essential to stay informed about the latest developments and price fluctuations.

stories.headline: Indian stock markets suffer losses as global tensions escalate
stories.summary: Indian stock markets extended losses for the third consecutive session, with Sensex and Nifty falling around 1% on April 24, 2026. The decline was driven by weak global cues, escalating tensions between Iran and the US, and surging oil prices. A weaker rupee and continued selling by foreign institutional investors (FIIs) also contributed to the cautious sentiment. Additionally, geopolitical tensions between India and Pakistan, following a terror attack in Jammu and Kashmir, have been weighing on investor sentiment. The combined market capitalisation of all BSE-listed companies witnessed a significant wipe-out of ₹10 lakh crore within the first two hours of trade on April 25, 2025. The broad-based selling pressure has led to a sharp sell-off in the Indian equity markets, with investors turning cautious amid rising global tensions.

stories.headline: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
stories.summary: China is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors. The bills aim to curb the flow of advanced artificial intelligence and semiconductor technologies to China. Beijing warned that if passed, these bills could disrupt global supply chains. The Chinese Ministry of Commerce stated that the US is increasingly using "national security" justifications to impose trade restrictions, which could undermine the international economic order. The move follows US efforts to slow China's semiconductor development through extensive export controls. China has expressed concern that the proposed measures could have significant implications for the global semiconductor industry and supply chains, and is watching the progress of the legal measures closely. The US has been trying to limit China's access to advanced technologies, citing national security concerns.

stories.headline: Asian air travel faces jet fuel crunch and disruptions
stories.summary: A jet fuel supply crunch is disrupting air travel in Asia, with record prices and supply curbs affecting key aviation hubs in Vietnam, Thailand, South Korea, and China. The shortage, partly caused by the Middle East conflict, has led to flight cancellations and higher fares for passengers. Airlines are responding by cutting flights, carrying extra fuel, and adding refuelling stops. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted nearly 21% of global seaborne jet fuel supply, forcing carriers to reduce flight schedules. As a result, Asian airlines are suspending routes and reducing flight frequencies, threatening to slow the region's tourism rebound ahead of the peak summer season. The surge in jet fuel costs is expected to continue, impacting air travel in the region.

stories.headline: Just what the doctor ordered: how AI could help China bridge the medical resources gap
stories.summary: China is integrating AI into its healthcare industry to bridge gaps in medical resource distribution and raise efficiency. Experts believe AI can help streamline medical work, inform disease-screening programs, and analyze healthcare records to anticipate demand. This can enable better planning of resources, such as predicting the number of heart attacks in a given area. AI tools can also assist doctors in evaluating scans and diagnosing diseases. However, there are concerns that over-reliance on AI helpers may lead to a decline in cognitive stamina among medical professionals. To address this, medical education experts and healthcare leaders are considering ways to combat the effect and ensure that AI is used to augment, rather than replace, human medical expertise. The use of AI in healthcare has the potential to significantly improve the distribution of medical resources in China.

stories.headline: Pick n Pay pilots new convenience brand in SA
stories.summary: Pick n Pay is piloting a new convenience brand, Pick n Pay GO, in South Africa. The first pilot store, called Skubu, has launched in Diepsloot, Johannesburg, offering essential grocery items at up to half the price of other stores. This format was first introduced in Botswana last year in partnership with Vivo Energy. The Skubu store provides a discount format with a reduced range of goods, similar to Boxer supermarkets in SA. Pick n Pay's own brand products are available at all stores, making it convenient for customers to find what they need. The company is also testing a similar format in Lagos, which opened in March. The pilot aims to provide affordable grocery options to customers, with the potential for further expansion if successful. The new brand is part of Pick n Pay's strategy to increase convenience and affordability for its customers.
